entry.title: Some of my thoughts about Red Hat <<The Open Organization>>
entry.content:
The benefit of Open Source and Open Organization

The most significant misunderstanding of open source is “Tom Sawyer” model[1] of collaborations: you stare at your own pieces of “cake”, and wish people could jump in and help you instead of helping each other to make the whole “cake” bigger so everyone could be benefit from it. Red Hat is the latter one: to be the catalyst in communities of customers, contributors, and partners creating better technology the open source way.

Being catalyst is an insightful thinking which need truly collaborations from different organizations who willing to give up their own “shrewdness” and work together. To be honest, I don’t know what’s the future of open source will be like, just like I don’t know when human could colonize Mars, but I firmly believe we will be there someday.

There may have an inaccurate metaphor in my humble opinion to support my belief, the open source way qualified sharing economy: connection is more important than ownership, collaborations make resource being shared to make the society more efficient. Think about Airbnb, Uber, Google, Facebook… they don’t own contents/properties, but they have the connections. Sharing software is build a community provide an open space/connections for people to share intellectual properties, ideas, contributions from different countries, corporations. It will definitely lower the cost of software development cost, making the products more creativity, make hard problems being solved, is about making the world more efficient, making the “cake” bigger in a holistic view.

Top Down vs Bottom Up

Top Down hierarchy works for traditional company which the environment and goal is stable enough and everyone need to do is just follow the “correct” order do it efficiently, But Bottom up hierarchy works for company need creativity, fast move which means the environment and goal cannot be easily foreseen.

This make me remind an example of the Chinese Civil War[2], The Communist Party of China(CPC) win the war against Kuomintang-led government(KMT), the reason could be explained by “Top Down vs Bottom Up” theory, KMT uses Top Down hierarchy which seems more efficiency, but it could be had a severe problem during the war when the communication in the hierarchy being blocked. But Bottom Up hierarchy is make sure everyone even poor peasants and workers understand what they fight for and why (farmland reform) and have the inner momentum to fight fiercely even there’s no one giving them order. (igniting their passion)

Creativity

Previously, my understanding of creativity is narrowed: I always thought creativity is only some scientists pops up some brilliant ideas, or win some new fundamental finding prize in science, or some big corporation invent some new intellectual patents. But I was wrong, actually creativity is everywhere, think about apple, each release of the iPhone, the new product blows everybody’s mind, and then everyone starts to follow, turn out being the number one of the industry need creativity. So as Red Hat, we being the number one company of open source industry, everything we do will impact the open source community, and if we being creative enough, that open source could have the chance to be more gleaming.

Freedom, Openness, Diversity

Freedom, Openness and Diversity is the catalyst of creativity. Imagine what if scientific research world would be like without freedom, openness and diversity: political interference will lead to scientific fraud, benefit-oriented interference will lead to we miss some potential evolutionary inventions. Think about MIT media labs which was originally formed by some marginalized but talented professors whose research is not that “beneficial enough”, and the MIT give them a chance to form their own lab and boom: touchscreen, electronic ink, scratch programming design for kids, artificial prosthesis, wearable device, GPS vehicle tracking system etc all comes from media lab which are incredible inventions. Imagine each countries’ scientist/engineer all incarcerated in their own countries, never had the chance to have an open discussion/conference. As second industrial revolution passed almost a hundred year, the scientific/engineering problems we are tackling are way more difficult and complicated than anything before, so having a freedom, openness, diversity environment to collaborations are important. And I have to admit, for this area, US as an ethnic melt pot is way more advanced than any other countries.

The reason why I want to work for Red Hat and in US

First, I want to explain couple reasons about why I want to work in US, especially in Raleigh.

  1. Work reason:
    1.1 My Kubernetes community background/experience is #sig-cli and #sig-api-machinery, I have some collaborations with Clayton, Jordan, David, Juan, and if I have the chance to face to face communicate with them, that would be more efficiency. Also, Clayton, Jordan, David they’re like my role models from all perspective, it would be really valuable even if I could have the chance to chatting with them during the lunchtime or casual time. Collaborate with brilliant people and observe their behaviors is a great way to improve yourself.

  2. Life reason:

    2.1 I have three cousins work and live in US, since the one-child policy[3], two of them are really close to me just like brother and sisters.

    2.2 Personality/culture influences, I’m kind of rebel since I was in school, I always want to stand up and speak up, but maybe due to the Chinese/Confucius culture influences, I don’t have many chances to do that. Sometimes Confucius philosophy is good, but there’s a couple of common bad habits I been observed during the 20 years I grew up in this land: 1. Lack of compassion and empathy 2. Hive mind, subordinate thinking 3. Put money above all. But I’m not saying US is the greatest, and china is horrible, US do has a lot of problems from my online observation on YouTube[4][5]. But one of my expat friend really inspired me by told me: “You have to be truly experienced two different cultures to be really open-minded and decide where’s the best place you want to fit in.”

    2.3 I love music and most of my favorite bands/singers are from US and there’re definitely fewer concerts in China here, Raleigh is close to Tennessee : )

Second, the reason I think Red Hat is a good work place for me.

‘“Everyone can build their own impact in the open organization”’ I vaguely remember there’s a sentence exist in the book and that was really fascinating to me. Life is not just about making money, taking care families(I mean future family not my divorced parents), building impact, be open-minded, helping others are more important to me… I love coding, but I’m not a Chinese nerdy guy who only can code, and I do have some other potential abilities like public speaking, volunteering, Chinese/Asian culture background… could be benefit company in the future.

But it’s not necessary in a short term

So I’m not trying to persuade you guys, I know there might be some cost prohibitive reasons for a none-senior engineer to relocate and I totally understand. I just want to make sure you guys understand my work/life expectations/goals and my potential values to make a comprehensive assessment. Brno is also a great place for me to work for couple years, I knew there’re also a lot of brilliant people there if there’re opportunities for me. At last, thank you for reading my long tedious maybe immature thoughts.

[1] https://opensource.com/business/09/9/tom-sawyer-whitewashing-fences-and-building-communities-online

[2] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chinese_Civil_War

[3] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/One-child_policy

[4] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Last_Week_Tonight_with_John_Oliver

[5] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Philip_DeFranco_Show

entry.title: 字符串匹配问题之KMP算法的引出
entry.content:
问题(类似leetcode28)

暴力解法

如果用暴力匹配的思路，一个循环遍历串S,每次递增加1，如果字串和P匹配则返回。m是S串长度，n是P串长度,因为S串每次下标加1移动，所以最多要比较m*n次,这里时间复杂度是O(m*n) 。

Knuth–Morris–Pratt(KMP) 算法的引入

从直觉上讲，如果ABC前三位匹配，后面我们可以直接比较后面的,像这样:

而不是暴力的逻辑S字符串应该向后移动一位,和P比较,KMP其实就是这样去比较的，但是可以直接让串P右移动3位即可，这是为什么呢?

如果我们假设P只需要移动一位就会有匹配

可以有

所以如果只移动一位，当且仅当匹配的三个元素都相同才可以,及这种情况 AAA BBB XXX 前三元素都相同才可以。

如果我们假设匹配只需要移动两位

所以如果字符串是ATA，或者ABA, AXA 这种形式，可以移动两位

这样我们就发现了一个规律

对于前n位匹配的字符串，如果存在前缀和后缀相等，那么移动:

len(n)- len(最长前后缀)

这样我们可以看出，只要算出当前匹配的P子串，的最长前后缀个数，就可以求出移动距离，而不需要每次让S串只移动1位去匹配了。

使用Next数组记录

这样我们如果利用一个数组去记录串P所有位置上的最长前后缀串长度，就可以在匹配的时候直接查找数组，而且不用回溯后移了。这里我们用一个Next数组来定义,Next数组定义如下:

entry.title: Dynamic programming 101
entry.content:
Dynamic programming 101

The fundamental of dp

  1. It should be recursive
  2. optimal substructure

https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%8A%A8%E6%80%81%E8%A7%84%E5%88%92

Fibonacci numbers

F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2)

It could be an recursive problem

It could be use memorization search

It could be also an dyanmic programming

Leetcode 198. House Robber

top-down

Recursive implementation

memorization search

down-top

0-1 BackPack

Given n items with size Ai, an integer m denotes the size of a backpack. How full you can fill this backpack?

If we have 4 items with size [2, 3, 5, 7], the backpack size is 11, we can select [2, 3, 5], so that the max size we can fill this backpack is 10. If the backpack size is 12. we can select [2, 3, 7] so that we can fulfill the backpack.

You function should return the max size we can fill in the given backpack.

Recursive:

memorization search:

dyanmic equation

Sum up

Most of the dyanmic programming should be discrete status, because memories in computer are discrete.

DataSet

entry.title: 需要完善的地方 2018
entry.content:
short term

  1. 算法
    1.1 动态规划 (做一个slide 分享)
    1.2 数据结构 (leetcode 练习)

  2. 音标,发音

  3. 词汇 (主要通过GRE 3000,经济学人,和youtube)

  4. Linux (RHCE 如果今年能考下来会比较好，应该用不了多长时间)

long term

  1. guitar practice
  1. 数学有几本书需要读

entry.title: 读书笔记:《Go语言编程》
entry.content:
《Go语言编程》读书笔记

顺序编程

闭包

同是静态类型语言的Go作用域和C语言类似，虽然变量在栈上(stack)还是堆上(heap)是由编译器决定，这点和C语言用户自己决定不一样，但是作用于访问还是类似的，比如如下代码:

但是golang支持闭包，所以这样是可以的:

闭包的简单定义是能够读取其他函数内部变量的函数,并且这里函数内的变量n一定是在堆上，这点C语言是没办法做到的，除非堆上malloc一个变量。

面向对象编程

类型系统

类型系统里比较重要的两个概念Pass By Reference和 Pass By Value。
在C语言里处理数组在函数传递的时候比较特别是Pass By Reference,其他对象都是Pass By Value 结构体里的数组也不例外，在C语言中如下是等价的

然后Go语言这点不太一样数组是Pass By Reference的。然后Go语言引入了一种叫做切片(Slice)的类型,这个有点像C的数组，切片和数组看起来十分形似，唯一的区别实在声明的时候：

另外数组可以转换成切片通过这个方法

Go语言有四种特殊的类型通常是看作Pass by Reference的

  1. 切片(Slice)
  2. 接口(Interface)
  3. Map
  4. Channel

结构体部分

匿名引用指针和值引用的区别

C++的虚基类引出区别,首先讲一下虚基类

在C++中如果基类A分支出两个子类B和C，然后孙子类D同时又继承了B和C,这时基类A中有有一个成员变量是一个公共的属性，比如department,而孙子类既然享受了继承的好处，但是如果想调用name的话编译器会又一个问题，就是不知道是用从那个分支继承下来的成员变量，这个时候如果这个department一个大家共用的属性,这样多重继承下来不仅有数据冗余同时还要每次显示告诉编译器使用从那个分支继承下来的变量，非常麻烦，C++在这里引入了虚继类的概念

这样这里的department其实就只有一份，不管哪里的修改都是对A的department进行修改，其实原理也很简单，就是用指针，然后大家都共享A变量的那一块内存就行了呗,Go在这里的实现非常的直白

结构体组合

匿名组合

接口

侵入性和非侵入性

不同于java, C++ 这类接口我们称为侵入式接口。
“侵入式”的主要表现在于实现类需要明确声明自己实现了某个接口。

这种强制性的接口继承是面向对象编程思想发展过程中
一个遭受相当多置疑的特性。

侵入式接口的两个问题

正是因为这种不合理的设计，实现Java、Csharp类库中的每个类时都需要纠结以下两个问题。
 问题1:我提供哪些接口好呢?
 问题2:如果两个类实现了相同的接口，应该把接口放到哪个包好呢?

接口赋值的一个代码的例子

  1. 接口包含两个方法其中一个值引用，一个地址引用，赋值变量应该用值还是地址?
  2. 接口方法之间赋值，只要是子集，就可以赋值,并且顺序无关

接口查询接口组合

只看类C代码实现

空接口 {}interface

可以做泛型编程而不需要空指针

并发编程 go routinue

反射

Type 和 Value

reflect.TypeOf()

reflect.ValueOf() //这里是值拷贝 可以用CanSet()函数来判断

所以要想得到理想的reflect工作流
需要首先 v = ValueOf(&x) 但是地址是不能直接Set的，要 v.Elem() 之后再Set()

entry.title: Kubernetes 架构 Overview
entry.content:
Master/Node 架构

Master相当于中心控制集群其中Master中的APIServer和etcd交流，Node相当于工作集群用来和docker daemon/runc 打交道实际操作管理容器，这里需要注意的是etcd是一个分布式的KV存储系统,支持WATCH接口,通过http long poll实现，使用Raft算法保证数据一致性。

Kubernetes实际模块功能

黄色的部分是运行在Master上的进程，绿色的部分是运行在Node上的进程,紫色的部分是以插件或者pod的形式运行。对于OpenShift,基本的架构都是复用Kubernetes，一部分不同的地方在于kubernets 有许多自己加入的Admission Controllers 注意这个和第三部分的Controller不是一个概念.当然OpenShift也有一些自己的controller 比如说Build Config, Build, Deployment Config 等等这些kubernetes所没有的功能。

Contronller 简介

在Kubernets/OpenShift中Controller 是一个非常重要的概念，它的定义其实很简单，就是一个 loop, 通过和APIServer通讯获取它想要的数据，然后比对用户目前想要达成的状态，如果者两者状态不一致，就做一些同步的操作，然后将结构在写回APIServer,其实主要是etcd里，因为在Kubernetes/OpenShift中，所有的资源都是以Json的形式持久华在etcd中，而etcd只和APIServer打交道。

这里有一个简单的controller 的example

Pod或Replication Controller创建的时序图

这里我画了两个时序图，关于一个Pod是如何创建的，一个RC是如果创建的，这里contoller mananger 是所有generic controller 的一个集合的进程(目前所有的kubernetes controller), 而Scheduler虽然不是一个generic 的controller,但是他的行为模式和controller 是没有区别的 Scheduler相关的代码路径。

Kubernetes 网络解决方案

首先，kubernetes Docker 在网络方面都没有提供完整的解决方案，对于在不同网段的两个Pod,他们之间是无法直接通讯的，所以这里有几种解决方案

1.Node桥接模式

docker0相当于一台交换机，而用一根网线把两个交换机桥接起来，这样所有在这个交换机上的设备就可以通信，但是缺点就是ARP风暴和安全问题

2.Node路由模式

这种模式需要更改所有Node和链路上所有路由的路由规则，而且每次增删Node都需要增删规则，如果有一套动态路由协议，并且集群都在一个大内网环境，这个网络方案还可行，如果在两个Node在公网上，这个方案无法实现。

3.隧道模式

对于公网上的Node如果网段不同只能通过隧道的方式解决，Kubernets本身不提供任何方案，OpenShift 通过Open vSwitch 来解决，我这里介绍了CoreOS的解决方案flannel,其实隧道原理都大同小异。

Kubernets Service 的原理介绍

首先，Kubernets/OpenShift 的Service是一个负载均衡的概念（或者反向代理)主要实现是使用iptables, iptables是通过写一些规则来转发数据包，而kube-proxy就是用来在Node上操作这些iptables.
这里需要注意的是: Service的ip是一个不存在的虚拟ip(仅仅适用与cluster内部),是ping不同的，因为它只存在于etcd里供APIServer和Kube-proxy查询修改iptables来做到数据包转发的作用。

如果需要对外访问，需要配置Service对应的LoadBalancer(这个由Issa基础设施提供，比如AWS,GCE都有相应的LoadBalancer)而OpenShift自己提供了一个router的资源来expose service，另外kubernets社区上有目前也有一个叫ingress的controller/资源来解决Service对外访问的问题。

其他未涉及的部分

1.Auth

2.Storage

3.CLI

4.Kube-Dns

5.各种Controller的具体功能

6.Scheduler 具体调度算法

7.Federation Cluster, Federation Service

8.Aggreator APIServer

9.HA Proxy

10.APIServer 多线程下的Optimistic Concurrency Control

11.Admission Controllers

12.etcd 的机制

13.等等

entry.title: 2017读书清单
entry.content:
今年读的一些书

  1. 迷人的材料
  2. Kubernetes权威指南
  3. Docker容器与容器云
  4. 人类简史
  5. Go语言编程
  6. 财富自由之路
  7. 智能时代
  8. Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea (还未看完)
  9. Originals: How Non-Conformists Move the World
  10. Go程序设计语言(大概看了一下)
  11. The Open Organization: Igniting Passion and Performance
  12. Design patterns for container-based distributed systems (tech paper)

还有一些零星的书和Paper没有列出来。还有两篇想读的Paper还没有看完

  1. bitcoin a peer-to-peer electronic cash system
  2. MapReduce: Simplified Data Processing on Large Clusters

很惭愧，因为今年重心全放在了工作，社区工作和GRE单词上,并没有读多少书….

不过今年估计最少又掌握了一些新单词(整理出了一个整本单词本),相比去年拙劣的考分，如果明年年初再考一次估计Verbal会有长远进步…

另外今年还听了一些书评，这里也顺便列出来，以后有空把这些原版给读了(优先级并不高,除了未来简史和另外两本*)

  1. Homo Deus: A Brief History of Tomorro
  2. Trying not to try: The Art and Science of Spontaneity
  3. Messy: The Power of Disorder to Transform Our Lives
  4. Algorithms to Live By: The computer Science of Human Decisions
  5. Success and Luck: Good Fortune and the Myth of Meritocracy
  6. The End of Average: How We Succeed in a World That Values Sameness
  7. The Attention Merchants: The Epic Scramble to Get Inside Our Heads
  8. Hit Makers: How Things Become Popular
  9. Stretch: Unlock the Power of Less- and Achieve More That You Ever Imagined
  10. The Complacent Class: The Self-Defeating Quest fro the American Dream
  11. Tools of Titans: The Tactics, Routines, and Habits of Billionaires, Icons, and World-Class Performers
  12. Sensemaking: The Power of the Humanities in the Age of the Algorithm
  13. Decisive: How to make better choices in life and work
  14. Option B: Facing Adversity, Building Resilience, and Finding Joy (Sheryl SandBerg)
  15. Everybody Lies: Big Data, New Data, and What the Internet Can Tell Us About Who We Really Are
  16. Barking Up the Wrong Tree: The surprising Science Behind Why Everything You Know About Success Is Wrong
  17. Astrophysics for People in a Hurry
  18. Stealing Fire: How Silicon Valley, the Navy SEALs, and Maverick Scientists Are Revolutionzing the Way We live and Work
  19. The Telomere Effect: A Rveolutionary Approach to Living Younger, Healthier, Longer (重点)
  20. Popular: The Power of Likability in a Status- Obsessed World
  21. Peak Performance: Elevate Your Game, Avoid Burnout, and Thrive with the New Science of Success (重点,感受颇深)

最后一些明年想读的书

一些数学书，逻辑书(刚从家带过来的基本)
超越感觉 批判性思维
Planet Google (之前买的口袋书，等公交或者地铁上读,纯粹刷一下阅读量)
经济学人 （全年期刊订阅)
